name: smith deadlift
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Bend at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight and chest up, and grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and lift the bar by extending your hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body.
Stand up straight, fully extending your hips and knees.
Lower the bar back down by bending at the hips and knees, maintaining control and keeping your back straight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward. Bend at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight and chest up, and grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Engage your core and lift the bar by extending your hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body. Stand up straight, fully extending your hips and knees. Lower the bar back down by bending at the hips and knees, maintaining control and keeping your back straight. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
